name: Claude Opus 4.8
summary: Anthropic's new flagship lands today on claude.ai, Claude Code, the API, and Cowork — tops GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic coding benchmarks and is ~4x less likely than 4.7 to leave flaws in code it writes; same price as 4.7 ($5/M in, $25/M out) but fast mode is now 3x cheaper, plus a research preview of dynamic workflows that spawns hundreds of parallel subagents for jobs too big for one context window

name: Gemini Spark
summary: Google's 24/7 personal AI agent rolls into beta — runs on a cloud VM that keeps working when your laptop is closed, schedules and chains multi-step tasks across Gmail/Docs, and uses MCP to act inside Canva, OpenTable, and Instacart at launch (Adobe, Spotify, GitHub, Notion, and Slack queued for summer); opens this week for U.S. Google AI Ultra subscribers

name: Emdash
summary: open-source desktop app for running coding agents in parallel — each one isolated in its own git worktree; supports 22 CLI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, Amp + 16 more), pulls tickets from Linear/Jira/GitHub Issues, and ships with a built-in Kanban board to track agent progress across tasks

name: Figma AI Agent
summary: purpose-built AI agent embedded in Figma's canvas — natural-language prompts generate and remix designs, automate rote work, and respect your design system; fire up multiple agents to do different tasks in parallel, plus new partnerships baking Claude Code and Codex into the design surface; rolling out in beta for Full seats on Pro/Org/Enterprise

name: AI Studio Android Builder
summary: Google AI Studio can now build full native Android apps from a single prompt — outputs production-quality Kotlin in the latest Jetpack Compose patterns, previews in a browser-based emulator (or sideloads to a phone via ADB), supports offline mode plus hardware sensors (GPS, Bluetooth, NFC, camera, accelerometer), and offers one-click publish to Google Play's Internal Test Track; no Android Studio install required

name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
summary: Google's new high-efficiency multimodal model — near-Pro level coding and reasoning at Flash-tier cost ($1.50/M input, $9/M output, 1M-token context); accepts text, image, audio, video, and PDF and is tuned for parallel agentic loops; generally available today across the Gemini API, AI Studio, Antigravity, and Vertex AI with no waitlist

name: Gemini Omni Flash
summary: Google's first unified multimodal generation model — accepts image, audio, video, and text in a single prompt and reasons across them to produce video you can keep editing through conversation; rolling out worldwide to Gemini app subscribers (AI Plus/Pro/Ultra) and the Flow creative tool, plus free access for YouTube Shorts and YouTube Create users

name: Cursor Composer 2.5
summary: Cursor's in-house coding model is aimed at long, tool-heavy sessions across files, terminal, and tests; the pitch is frontier-style coding performance at a lower cost inside the editor where Cursor already has context
